news.headline: Italy Says It Recovered $23 Million Stolen From Ursula Andress, First ‘Bond Girl’
news.summary: The Swiss actress Ursula Andress, whose breakthrough came in “Dr. No” in 1962, accused a former wealth manager of embezzlement. He died in an apparent suicide last year.

news.headline: Projectile from Lebanon kills at least one in north Israel
news.summary: Video captured flames and black smoke where a projectile from Lebanon killed at least one person in northern Israel.

news.headline: Costa Rica to accept 25 deportees per week under Trump deportation effort
news.summary: The Central American nation is latest to sign a 'third-country' deportation agreement with the Trump administration.

news.headline: Trump ‘pausing’ plan to destroy Iranian power plants for 10 days
news.summary: Donald Trump says he is “pausing” his plan to destroy Iranian power plants for 10 days.

news.headline: Trump says U.S. to hold off for 10 days on hitting Iran energy sites
news.summary: Trump’s statement follows an Iranian report that it had rejected a 15-point U.S. ceasefire proposal, a move that still left the door open for a counteroffer.

news.headline: Mbappe nets for 10-man France in win against Brazil in World Cup warm-up
news.summary: France beat Brazil 2-1 in a friendly in United States, in what was a potential dress rehearsal for World Cup 2026 final.

news.headline: Manila’s streets empty as fuel prices surge amid Strait of Hormuz crisis
news.summary: Empty streets expose another crisis as Filipinos battle soaring oil prices, dwindling incomes, and a stagnating economy.

news.headline: Malaysia Says Iran Will Allow Its Ships to Pass Through the Strait of Hormuz
news.summary: The reprieve would ease disruptions in Malaysia’s energy supply, but the prime minister has vowed to make preparations for a more volatile future.

news.headline: Why Iraq is the most fragile front in the US-Israel war on Iran
news.summary: Iraq is under attack from all sides as militias and foreign powers clash.
